perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement the term confidential information includes proprietary materials and information relating thereto including without limitation specifications , drawings, designs, computer software and knowhow. In general the receiving party must afford disclosed confidential information the same degree of protection as it would afford its own.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement the term confidential information is limited to specifications and drawings explicitly marked as confidential. In general the receiving party must afford disclosed confidential information the same degree of protection as it would afford its own.
perturbation.explanation: This change narrows the definition of 'confidential information' to only include specifications and drawings marked confidential. This creates a contradiction because later sections of the document refer to confidential information more broadly, including oral disclosures and know-how, which would no longer be covered under this narrower definition. This in-text contradiction is particularly problematic in Section 3 which discusses the handling of 'all confidential information (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record)'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party will receive all confidential in formation (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record) which is discl osed in connection with this Agreement subject to the following conditions: a) it will take all proper and reasonable measures to ensure that the confidentiality of such information is maintained; b) it will not use the information for any commercial purpose or manufacture without obtaining a written licence or other agreement from the disclosing party; c) it will not disclose the information to any third party without written permission; d) it will not disclose the information to employ ees other than those above except to the extent necessary to fulfil the purposes set out above and all such other employees to which it will disclose it will be made aware of the confidential nature of the information, and the conditions of disclosure herein; e) it will not make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission from the disclosing party; f) it will acknowledge the source (i.e. one of the organisations signatory to this Agreement) of, and will mark "Confidential", any drawing, document or software incorporating the information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'confidential information' to only include specifications and drawings explicitly marked as confidential. The contradicted text, however, refers to 'all confidential information (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record)'. This creates a direct conflict because the original text covers a broader range of information, including oral disclosures and know-how, which are now excluded by the changed definition. This makes the handling and protection of orally disclosed confidential information, as stipulated in the contradicted section, inconsistent and potentially unenforceable, as these are no longer considered 'confidential information' under the new definition.

perturbation.original_text: The parties will mark or otherwise designate confident ial information to show expressly or by necessary implication that it is imparted in confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties *may* mark or otherwise designate confidential information to show expressly or by necessary implication that it is imparted in confidence.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces 'will' with 'may,' making the marking of confidential information optional. This introduces ambiguity because the receiving party's obligations in later sections are predicated on knowing what information is confidential. If marking is optional, it's unclear how the receiving party is to know what information falls under the agreement, especially given the definition of confidential information including oral disclosures as described in Paragraph 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party will receive all confidential in formation (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record) which is discl osed in connection with this Agreement subject to the following conditions: a) it will take all proper and reasonable measures to ensure that the confidentiality of such information is maintained; b) it will not use the information for any commercial purpose or manufacture without obtaining a written licence or other agreement from the disclosing party; c) it will not disclose the information to any third party without written permission; d) it will not disclose the information to employ ees other than those above except to the extent necessary to fulfil the purposes set out above and all such other employees to which it will disclose it will be made aware of the confidential nature of the information, and the conditions of disclosure herein; e) it will not make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission from the disclosing party; f) it will acknowledge the source (i.e. one of the organisations signatory to this Agreement) of, and will mark "Confidential", any drawing, document or software incorporating the information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that parties 'will' mark confidential information, creating a mandatory obligation. The changed text replaces 'will' with 'may,' making it optional. The contradicted text states 'The receiving party will receive all confidential in formation...subject to the following conditions,' which includes obligations based on the receiving party knowing what is confidential. If marking is optional, as stated in the Changed Text, it creates a direct contradiction because the receiving party's ability to fulfill its obligations under the contradicted text is now uncertain, leading to ambiguity and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party will receive all confidential in formation (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record) which is discl osed in connection with this Agreement subject to the following conditions: a) it will take all proper and reasonable measures to ensure that the confidentiality of such information is maintained;
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party will receive all confidential in formation (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record) which is discl osed in connection with this Agreement subject to the following conditions: a) it will take reasonable measures to ensure that the confidentiality of such information is maintained, where reasonably possible;
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'where reasonably possible', the obligation to maintain confidentiality becomes subjective and less stringent. This contradicts the general intent of the agreement to protect confidential information, as the receiving party could argue that maintaining confidentiality was not 'reasonably possible' in various situations. This impacts the enforceability of Paragraph 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties agree that at all times, during and after the current discussions, and thereafter for a period of ten (10) years, starting from the effective date of this Agreement, not to communicate or to divulge to third parties confidential information received from the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the receiving party 'will take all proper and reasonable measures'. The changed text modifies this to 'will take reasonable measures...where reasonably possible'. This introduces a subjective element, weakening the obligation to maintain confidentiality. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that 'Both parties agree that at all times...not to communicate or divulge' confidential information. The addition of 'where reasonably possible' allows the receiving party to potentially disclose confidential information if they deem it not reasonably possible to maintain confidentiality, which is in direct conflict with the absolute restriction on disclosing such information in the 'Contradicted Text'. This falls under 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: it will not use the information for any commercial purpose or manufacture without obtaining a written licence or other agreement from the disclosing party;
perturbation.changed_text: it will not use the information for any commercial purpose or manufacture *in direct competition with* the disclosing party without obtaining a written licence or other agreement from the disclosing party;
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a condition that restricts commercial use only if it's in direct competition with the disclosing party. This weakens the protection of confidential information, as the receiving party could use the information for commercial purposes that don't directly compete with the disclosing party, thereby potentially undermining Paragraph 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties agree that at all times, during and after the current discussions, and thereafter for a period of ten (10) years, starting from the effective date of this Agreement, not to communicate or to divulge to third parties confidential information received from the other party.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided broad protection of confidential information by preventing its use for any commercial purpose. The changed text weakens this protection by only restricting commercial use that directly competes with the disclosing party. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that confidential information received from the other party shall not be communicated or divulged to third parties at any time, creating an ambiguity about what constitutes permissable use of confidential information, and thus a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Under the terms of this Agreement there is no explic it or implied transfer of ownership to the receiving party of any drawings, documents or softwar e, or the copyright subsisting in them.
perturbation.changed_text: Under the terms of this Agreement there is no explic it transfer of ownership to the receiving party of any drawings, documents or softwar e, or the copyright subsisting in them.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'implied' from 'explicit or implied transfer of ownership,' the agreement opens the possibility of an implied license or right to use the disclosed information for purposes not explicitly transferred but reasonably inferred from the disclosure context, which contradicts the intent of Paragraph 3b, restricting commercial use.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party will receive all confidential in formation (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record) which is discl osed in connection with this Agreement subject to the following conditions: b) it will not use the information for any commercial purpose or manufacture without obtaining a written licence or other agreement from the disclosing party;
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibited both explicit and implied transfers of ownership. The changed text removes the 'implied' prohibition, now only preventing explicit transfers. The contradicted text (Paragraph 3b) restricts the receiving party from using the information for any commercial purpose without a written license. By removing the prohibition on implied transfers, the changed text creates a possibility of an implied license or right to use the disclosed information for purposes not explicitly transferred, but reasonably inferred from the disclosure context, potentially enabling commercial use without a written license, contradicting Paragraph 3b.

perturbation.original_text: Both parties agree that at all times, during and after the current discussions, and thereafter for a period of ten (10) years, starting from the effective date of this Agreement, not to communicate or to divulge to third parties confidential information received from the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: Both parties agree that at all times, during and after the current discussions, and thereafter for a period of ten (10) years, starting from the effective date of this Agreement, not to communicate or to divulge to third parties confidential information received from the other party, *unless required by law*.
perturbation.explanation: Adding the phrase 'unless required by law' introduces a potentially broad exception to the confidentiality obligation. This is an in-text contradiction because it undermines the strength and clarity of the overall non-disclosure agreement and could be interpreted broadly to allow disclosures under circumstances not originally intended to be exempt. This addition significantly weakens the protection initially promised in the agreement, particularly regarding what constitutes a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The receiving party will receive all confidential in formation (whether recorded in writing or by other means or given orally without record) which is discl osed in connection with this Agreement subject to the following conditions: a) it will take all proper and reasonable measures to ensure that the confidentiality of such information is maintained; b) it will not use the information for any commercial purpose or manufacture without obtaining a written licence or other agreement from the disclosing party; c) it will not disclose the information to any third party without written permission; d) it will not disclose the information to employ ees other than those above except to the extent necessary to fulfil the purposes set out above and all such other employees to which it will disclose it will be made aware of the confidential nature of the information, and the conditions of disclosure herein; e) it will not make any copy of or abstract of the information without specific written permission from the disclosing party; f) it will acknowledge the source (i.e. one of the organisations signatory to this Agreement) of, and will mark "Confidential", any drawing, document or software incorporating the information.
perturbation.justification: The original text creates a strict confidentiality obligation. The changed text introduces 'unless required by law', which creates a large exception. The contradicted text requires taking 'all proper and reasonable measures to ensure that the confidentiality of such information is maintained' and says 'it will not disclose the information to any third party without written permission.' The 'unless required by law' exception directly contradicts the requirements in the contradicted text because it permits disclosure to a third party without permission under certain circumstances, and undermines taking 'all proper and reasonable measures' to maintain confidentiality since disclosure is now permitted under law, thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the explanation provided and satisfying the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
